rules.description: For topology Reversi, replace fixed corner/X-square concepts with graph anchors, low-degree regions, stable rays, frontier exposure, and mobility on the actual adjacency graph.
Do not apply WTHOR standard-board openings directly on torus, cylinder, polar, Mobius, RP2, 3D, or 4D boards; use self-play books and transfer mobility, frontier, stability, and parity features only.
On no-boundary boards, stable structure can come from complete wrap cycles, closed rings, low-frontier regions, and mutually protected rays rather than ordinary corners.

For torus, cylinder, Klein, RP2, 3D, and 4D boards, penalize early moves that flip many discs into exposed frontier regions unless they create anchors, cycles, or forced mobility loss for the opponent.

On curved or embedded boards, bracket rays must follow the legal graph directions, not visual shortcuts across the drawing. Moves that appear close in screen space but are not connected by the board graph should be ignored.
For wrapped boards, endgame search should split empty regions by actual graph components and cycle seams before applying parity; rendered boundaries are not necessarily endgame regions.
